This review is of course, of my personal views of the game and its current state, from some of the gameplay mechanics and my critiques for things that could enhance the gameplay, to starting gameplay strategies for new players. I have been waiting quite a while for this game to make it out of alpha. The new mechanics are quite awesome as is the new progression system. very solid skill based combat as well as many hours of gameplay grinding out good loot solo, or with friends. Overall, this game in its current state is great, hopefully with some great future content updates! This game could certainly use a tutorial system for newer players, as veterans already know the ropes, the newcomers do not. I personally have mixed feelings about region locked gear, I enjoy the fact that it challenges the player to complete a zone to grind out some level gear to move onto other zones. This would be okay, if finding higher level gear had a higher chance of dropping as a plus variant. As the game has limitless lands to explore, not being able to get good gear and not being able to take it with you to aid you in exploring new zones. Something I'd personally like to see is a shop where you could take your best gear and pay to make it a grade piece of armor, an accessory, or weapon. Or even a variant that allows you to take it with you anywhere, anytime. My second thing i have mixed feelings about regarding Region Locked Gear is the fact that your items such as the Hang Glider, Boat, and Reigns to name a few. Are also region locked, It would be nice to either have the region lock for these items totally removed, or modified to allow some or all of the special items to be taken anywhere, but at the price of needing to explore more places, or complete quests. Overall, This game has a certain charm, which is why I have stuck around for as long as I have. This game deserves more praise for what has been accomplished thus far, as the Development Team is comprised of a husband and wife. The fact that 2 people do the coding, pixel art, music, and overall design for the game is inspiring. They should definitely be rewarded for their work more than they have. Of course, some people are mad the game wasn't regularly updated, or teased. But with a small Dev team it's to be expected. but back to the criticism and such. This game is unique, it's simplistic blocky design mixed with a pretty solid, skill based combat system makes for a pretty good game. Although, some of the new features added with the steam release kinda nullify the skill based combat, as lower level weapons do not deal sufficient damage if any until you achieve a combo streak to overtake higher level enemies. This game as it stands right now, is certainly very fun, and if you give it a small chance, and a few hours into the game to really get a feel for the flow, the mechanics, and the combat system. You'll have a great time. People have mentioned that its like a "walking simulator" which may be true in some way, but that's part of the exploration. Having to go out and explore to find new, previously unknown locations is quite fun. As sometimes you're pleasantly surprised, or to your dismay. Find treasure such as higher level armor, weapons, or accessories. Or otherwise, a world boss that wants nothing more than to beat you to a pulp. But as progression goes, as of now its more of a weapon-based leveling system. The standard rarity colors apply to this game as well. Common, uncommon, rare, epic, and legendary. which are White, Green, Blue, Purple, and Yellow respectively. You start with low-level white gear, which is effective against white enemies. Or if you have spent enough time, can be useful against Green enemies granted you get yourself a multi-hit combo going. The more hits you get in a combo, the more damage each hit will do. This isn't infinite however, there is a damage cap to the weapon and the combo you get with it. But to start, use your base gear to fight white enemies and get a feel for the combat, then attempt to fight green enemies to try your hand at getting higher level loot. If you get knocked down, you'll respawn at the last Shrine of Life you activated. Always keep this in mind when fighting higher level enemies, as you don't want to trek 100 miles to where you were last fighting. Keep an eye out for small white structures with a little statue in the middle. But that's pretty much it! This game as it stands for me is still solid in its design. And for me at least, it had me in my seat for almost 7 hours straight. If you like challenging Open World, Voxel based, Exploration RPG's. This game is for you, although there are some clunky and odd mechanics, as it stands its playable enough to have hours of fun in decently long sittings. I hope this review was helpful!

I love this game more than I would have thought, especially if someone described it as "SimCity where the citizens only mildly listen to you." The two essential knobs you have are zoning, job assignment, and a little bit of trade. You will sell your soul for "stone," because one it runs dry, the game effectively grinds to a halt Playing this game has given me a newfound respect for the project managers at work, since 100 of the game is resource allocation and priorities under harsh deadlines. Sure, you i want i a school, but are you willing to pull one of the blacksmiths off of making much needed tools in order to have a teacher who does nothing until a 10 year old child arrives? Well, are you, punk? Pros - highly unique gameplay - high, high replayability - very pretty - Steam Workshop! Cons - only parts of the UI operate at any velocity, made worse by high resolution displays - lots of random noises, chickens, the clink of pixaxes, that kind of thing which one cannot disable without mods 10 10 would buy again

I got this game in a Humble Bundle monthly choice, and this is exactly the kind of game I like those bundles for. The kind of game that I'd never have found on my own but end up really enjoying. I normally don't like point and click adventures, but this one is an exception, mostly because of gameplay mechanic stuff. For one, I don't have to actually click on every part of the screen to find out what's interactable, as the cursor changes when I hover over objects I can interact with and changes to something else when I hover over directions I can go. This makes it a lot less frustrating. I also found the puzzles to be much more intuitive and make much more sense than other point and clicks. And even though the art style is pixely, you have the option for the font to be smooth and readable, which I really appreciated. The story was definitely engaging as well. There's danger and suspense, and interesting characters who are sympathetic, as well as occasionally funny without trying too hard. Of course, it's easy to sympathize with characters in this scenario, so we don't get really any backstory on any of them except Cate, and even that is only to the degree that it directly relates to the plot . It was interesting how the story plays out through the game, slowly revealing what's really going on up until the very end. I wasn't really satisfied with the ending, though. However, I read that the 'true ending' isn't until you play it through again, so I probably will do that.

Now I'm not sure what all these negative reviews hope to achieve other than helping to make sure this game never sees the light of day. Yes mistakes HAVE been made, it's their first attempt at a game..since Veteran developer's with way more experience make mistakes, as we all do, I think we can cut them some slack. They are still trying to give us a game that no veteran developer's are making FOR the PC. A veteran company that could easily get this moving faster and much easier..but they're not. I hear a lot of "this game is a scam".. Now to say that Identity is a great idea isn't a lie and I'm almost positive we can all If you actually like games agree on that..maybe these guys shouldn't have tried such a massive undertaking for their first project..but no other major developer with their own resources are giving PC gamer's TRIPLE A games sepcifically made for the PC on this scale..but if you want something done in this world you gotta do it yourself. Now if you have been thru business education, and I have then you know, it's all about making money..every business venture isn't a scam it's a quest to make money and some dont make it..doesn't mean it was a scam. Yes there are scammers out there but, just as being a PC gamer doesn't mean you automatically want to cheat, trying to get a game created and failing doesnt mean it was a scam It's a business venture first and foremost..a way to incorporate what they love doing the most and making money. I believe they have a great idea, I believe they bit off more than they can chew with what they hope to achieve. I believe the the very ppl that they are depending on to help fund it, and believe in their vision ,are the very ppl who are going to ruin it's chances of ever seeing full release. All because there are so many ppl who believe this is actually a scam..which means they never planned to deliver said game. Which is insane as they are much cheaper ways of scamming ppl out of money..like making a cheesey broken little game and selling skins and cosmetics..sound familiar? Fornite, PubG, Fallout 76..The list goes on and on. I do not think it's really fair that companies who are well established veteran developer's with infinite resources are selling us broken game, half complete games selling the other half as DLC, and many other mind f cks and no one says "Hey you guys have been shafting us for years, what's going on?" But these guys are trying to make a game that none of those developer's are trying to bring FOR the PC..and they are trying to do it with so much less resources, and somehow ppl expect them to be on the same schedule or better than these veteran developer's? Hmm less than 2 million dollars does not a genre defining PC game make..Yoda says.. If you believe in their idea for this game then support it. If you listen to all the ppl who do NOT understand the proccess and wait till further along in development..it might never make it. I'm tired of playing simulation games, that simulate one thing, and games that I already played on console, I want a game that's worthy of this "master race" I keep hearng about. A life simulator..GTA meets The Sims..but even better. Why would they want to scam us with a great idea like this..it would be like the investors of the very first GTA thinking it was a scam.. Major companies are making games and selling them to us at the beta stage and make us pay to beta test them, not to mention sell us stupid skins and outifts for more than the game cost us! They've turned gaming into Vegas with No Jackpot..if they can pull that crap and get away with it for years for the games I've played over the years..then I will "take one for the team" and help try and get a game made that can show ppl what PC games are capable of, and how high the bar should actually be set for the game companies selling PC games. Last time I checked..since a game like this has never been made so there's no set number or ball park figure as to the cost to make it, logic would dictate that it will take some time and cost a lot of money. Considering they are extremely low in number with little experience, money would only help them hire more seasoned programmer's, designer's, artist, etc..which in turn would get the game developed. Is it this new generation, that is used to having everything right in front of them, that somehow expects that they dictate how long and how much it should cost before they deem it a scam..that's the future of what games get made if they dont have their own capital..sad face emoticon.. I think it's extremely unfair to wait years for console game's, a fraction of the size of what Identity plans to encompass, these developer's have their own resources with all the manpower they need. Yet ppl expect Asylum to create a FOR PC game that's bigger than what the veteran's are making FOR CONSOLE and ppl expect them do it with what..hope and aspirations? If they get the money needed to pay the ppl making it, and other ppl needed to do things a game hasn't done before, then it would get made, but it still takes time..which only gives them the opportunity to prove themselves and make lots of money which is the goal of a business.

I bought the five anniversary package on steam before Christmas, and it was my first game in this platform. You can identify how important it is to me. Kan Gao and freebird Games, thank you for your game design and story. I played it exactly on 25 Dec 2016 for the whole Christmas day, and read the English dialogue carefully word by word, and make notes on them, including the whole words between John and River when they met and chatted under the moon. Because of the careful reading and making notes, it actually took me more than 13 hours to go over the whole game, until 6 am on 26 Dec. I am totally moved by this game, even though I heard of this game for years and knew it is affecting That is why it is my first steam game . But still, when I really went through the game, I felt I recalled a number of forgotten things in my own life, just like John's oblivion. This plot is about love, amnesia, autism, and telling to each other. The theme of love includes many dimensions the obscure love between John and River when they was a kid, the love between them after the marriage but John has forgotten the past, the eternal love between them engraved by the lighthouse and even built real graves under it, the metaphor of lighthouse about communication and love, the expression of love and reminder by River of origami rabbits.. I suppose, besides love, the meaning of life and the importance of forgotten things are the most important elements.I just started the game without any background knowledge of the game, expect for the game's goodness. As the plot went on, it was just like a touching movie to me. There are no fight, no level and no 'death' in this game, instead, it has humorous dialogue between two doctors, impressive life development of John, and more importantly, the consideration of meaning of life. The game is also a detective plot, letting player to find out the final answer 'why he want to go to the moon' by flash-back of John's whole life. As I describe, when I read the dialogues in reversed scenes, it recalled me my own life experience, just like what we did in the exploration of John's life. I know the game is still being put together, but i hope it's hardly done because so much is missing. All the features we all know and love from RCT3 like peep designer, etc. are all gone. Hardly a fraction of the flatrides, water rides, pools, coasters, and transport rides are not in the game. The new grid system really takes some getting used to. You can't edit the land square in line with the grid anymore which is disapointing. The peeps walk around with less intelligence than the one's from RCT2. All in all, the game needs drastic amounts of new and old features put into it. I know the cliffhanger ride, the tilt-awhirl, the tumble bug, caterpillar, and the kangaroo have never been in any of the other versions, but id really love to see them in this game. The fact that you cant build custom buildings and walls like in RCT3 makes me angry. I don't like how que line entrances and exits are pre-attatched to the rides. There are so many limits in this game that should be broken through. My imagination is stressed out from running into all these things I can't do which I should be able to. Why can't I build tunnels anymore? On a brighter note, I do love the fact that some of the flatrides left out of all the previous versions are finally in the game, and the newer features such as the variable paths. I will keep this review updated.

You start expecting the worst before the draft even ends. A lot of matches feel like solo queue teammates get roasted in chat when someone sounds new. There are still way too many games where players with accents hear open hate the second they speak and nobody pushes back. The gameplay loop keeps pulling people in, but the player culture keeps pushing them right back out. I notice it most in public lobbies.

Even when PUBG squads is playable, the social atmosphere is rough. Plenty of games are stressful without becoming this ugly. A lot of women avoid comms because as soon as the lobby hears a female voice, the creepy comments begin immediately. Match chat gets flooded with abuse that isn't even about the match. At some point the community becomes harder to deal with than the actual game.

With almost 300 hours played, and having played the original game and the 2 expansions Hearts of Stone and Blood Wine , I feel now it's the time for my Witcher 3 review It's simply the best open world RPG I have ever played, and it will be hard to top it. - The main arc is a well elaborated and powerful story with plenty of choices that can determine how you navigate through the game and which ending you achieve. - The two expansions are gold, especially Blood Wine. They are definitely worth playing. And on a personal note, Blood Wine seems the perfect ending to the Witcher saga. - Bang for your buck If you are a completionist you would probably spend at least 150 hours playing. If you get to purchase the game 2 expansions for 15 EUR or less that means each hour of gameplay is 0.1 EUR. - If you finish the game and still want more you have the option to play the game again, but starting with the level, skills and inventory you finished the game with. There is a couple new additions to the skill tree that make this second go even more rewarding because now you play 100 of the game with the skills of a proper Witcher. - Character development is top notch. Every character has its motivations and they are consistent throughout the game - Geralt's skill tree is very broad so you can have different experiences of playing depening on how you choose to assign your skill points when levelling up. - The graphics are amazing and the game does not feel old. You can play it in the highest resolutions and still be amazed at the level of detail, the light effects.. - The soundtrack is immersive. With a nice headset you will feel you are in the game and will lose track of time. - Gwent it has one of the best games within a game ever designed. Playing Gwent is so fun that you might travel just to play a couple of card games against worthy rivals. To sum up if you like open world RPG buy it. If you don't buy it too. If the game expansions are on sale for 15 EUR or below don't hesitate.

Great game, but incredibly difficult and reminiscent of older games like Ghost 'n Goblins. If you love those old games, you'll love this. This is a game that is almost entirely skill-based. If you want a game that will hold your hand you aren't going to get it here. Every enemy you encounter has a specific way of being defeated that will often require you to have a solid grasp of the fighting and platforming mechanics. There's a limited save system that allows you to resume roughtly where you left off, but you'll need to collect lives in-game--and likely play the game in one-sitting--to beat the optional later levels and get the best ending. That might seem a bit too harsh, but once you get the core gameplay down you'll be able to get to the final boss in about an hour. In short Difficult, but extremely fun, and with good core gameplay that's difficult without feeling cheap.

This is my favorite game. I haven't played a huge number of games yet, but I've played a considerable variety - even though I'm sure I'll discover a lot more of amazing games in the future, and even though I already know of several more I love, this one is really special to me for several reasons. For one, the story is really impactful to me, and gave me a different perspective on some things that I hadn't considered as much. There's a specific situation with that that I'll mention somewhat generally in spoiler text. Spoiler tag because I think it might be better to experience it on your own first and see what you think about it, instead of knowing that there's some general thing I thought was good coming with a specific part of the story. spoiler The relationship of the main character with a sort of metaphorical character called Part of Her is developed really well, in my opinion. And it gave me some insight about myself as well. spoiler There are a lot of moments in the story that I really love in addition to this - I might add some actual spoiler sections to the review later, but for now, I'll leave it fairly general. And then, the difficulty has been a huge positive for me - I really admire that the difficulty is whatever you want it to be many of the more difficult things are optional, and you can go back to get them later if you decide you don't want to the first time and there's an optional, highly configurable assist mode to make the game accessible to whatever your needs and preferences are , and for me, I needed a challenging game. This game has helped me as I've worked through some difficult things in my life, which has definitely done a lot for my attachment to it - and what I needed for that was a challenge to run at again and again until I finally did it, until I could show myself there was something I could do, there was a challenge I could surmount, there was something tangible I could be good at. Of course that kind of accomplishment is no replacement for the things we need to do in real life, but it can be a meaningful supplement when things are confusing and difficult and you want to see that you i can i overcome something hard. The difficulty of this game was crafted in such a way that it's clear the game - or, more realistically, the developers - i want i you to succeed. Whatever that means for you. Those two things combined made a great experience for me, and the way it was done gave me more of an attachment to it than even my other favorite games have. There are things I really like in those that are similar to my reasons here, but they aren't done in quite this way, with those two things together. And not every game has to be like this, or have that kind of attachment - but because it does is why I can say it's my favorite.

I feel it necessary to post my own review of this game, which seems to be panned for its inability to run on every PC out there. This is NOT RCT2 or even RCT3. But it IS a nice game with great graphics and a very challenging Story Mode. I am an avid, Longtime PC gamer 25 years . I've played every Rollercoaster Tycoon game, and loved them. I STILL play RCT3 with my children. I am VERY critical of games that don't meet up to their billing. This new Iteration is NOT deserving of the bashing it is getting. Yes, the computer requirements are steep. If you just meet the Minimum requirements, you will have a Minimum game experience. Don't put this game on the Schlock list just because your computer isn't kept "Latest Greatest". The Story Mode gives you a goal with a challenging start point, and you move from scenario to scenario as you complete the tasks for each level. I like the "Tycoon Points" for purchasing new designs technologies, as it adds more depth than the previous games had. There IS a sandbox mode, where you can stretch your legs and get used to the interface and perfect your coaster-building before wasting a lot of cash in the Story Mode. You can USE the Tutorial in there to walk thru the various elements of gameplay, and learn just how different it is from previous RCT games it is. Ther may ber a few, minor hassles like dropping a mechanic at a broken ride, then watching him walk completely away from it just to go check on a working ride. But that doesn't warrant a Terrible review. My recommendation Test Drive it for yourself. Steam guarantees your satisfaction, right? Purchase and play it for an hour. That will let you see if your PC can run the game. Plus, it will give you a quick look into the interface and the Story Mode. If it sucks to you, get your money back, but don't go Flaming it on the reviews.

To the Moon review In depth spoiler free analysis "To the Moon is a thoroughly engrossing game about life, loss, and love. Set in a near future where memories can be accessed and rewritten, we follow two scientists as they attempt to rewrite the memories of a dying man to fulfill his wish of traveling to the moon. Because of this "To the Moon" grapples with some pretty complex issues. Can we really trust memories considering how inaccurate they are? Memories shape who we are as people, but are they themselves shaped and distorted by later biases? What actually constitutes as a happy successful life? Can it be considered ethical at all to rewrite an entire person s life? I was surprised by just how dirty I felt traveling back into this guy s head, and the casual way the two scientists play with a dying man s last thoughts. It felt as though I was watching something private and sacred that never should have been witnessed to begin with. This goes a long way to reinforce the game s themes and the mentality that this is just a job to these people do. At one point one of them even goes as far as to say they re just doing their job and it s not not their right "to play God," further blurring the lines of some already murky issues. This game never has to sink to clunky exposition as the rules of this world are effortlessly woven into the gameplay and superb environmental design. Not to mention the powerful music and wonderful ambiance of the game. I love how during especially dark moments the soundtrack will cut out entirely and all you hear is the distant wheezing of the machine keeping the man alive. It s incredibly creepy and really sells the intensity of the scene. It all just works so well. What doesn t work however is the banter. I m going to be brutally honest, the banter almost killed the game for me. I respect the author s choice to include levity as it helps keep some seriously dark subject matter from ever becoming too gloomy, however it often felt incredibly forced and awkward. Especially when they drop the act and you get to see these scientists work to solve a complex problem using the game s own internal logic. It s extremely entertaining and I really wish I could have seen more of that. My only real concern is the game s third act. While I won t spoil anything, I will say it feels a bit rushed. The climax features some incredibly suspenseful twists that could have recontextualized the whole game if they had just given them a little more time to breath and a lot less banter. And while some of the game s more psychological themes aren t fully delivered upon, the questions they pose stuck with me for days after completing the game. To the Moon was a little light on actual gameplay which may be a turn off for some, however the story alone was enough to keep me engaged. I would strongly encourage you to play it in one sitting as it can be beaten in an afternoon. While a little rushed towards the end, To the Moon is an infinitely ambitious, and thoroughly engaging tale that is well worth the price of admission. 4 5 stars, I would certainly recommend it.

First of all, there are two things you should take into account, before buying Atlas. It's an EA and a Survival Game. So you shoundn't be surprised, if you die with a raft on open sea and some major bugs are still a thing. After this I want to look deeper into the game. At first the grahics are incredible and nearly bug free. The Audio is good and changes dynamicly with the regions and your actions, but the moaning of the character, when it's hungry, is really annoying and needs to be fixed. The overall performance is good and the game runs even better than ARK on my PC. However there are stil lags in the game, when you are changing the server region. The Shipbuilding is excellent, you can configurate your Ship with different planks, sails and canons, although four ship types for a pirate game are not enough. The skill system with different trees fit very well with the Teamplay and even in late game you can't build all and the taming is more difficult than in ARK, but needs far less time. All in all I can recommend ATLAS to all fans of Survival Games and the Age of Piracy. PS I know my English isn't the best.

I wish there was a middle of the road option here. Co-Op is great fun, and the open levels are great to poke around for secrets. Props to Arkane for nailing level design. The RPG-lite stuff is kind of..meh? It's pretty clear which areas you're supposed to go through first just by how quickly you get mowed down by enemies, but it really puts a dampener on the whole "open world map" thing. But man, whatever you do, don't play this game in single player. Encounters are 100 designed around having two people actively fighting the AI doesn't really do a good job of that, if it's even in the area and you will feel every bit of health bar bloat when a generic Nazi trooper takes half a mag of headshots to put down. All in all, if you're looking for a fun, cheap co-op game, this is it, but stay away if you're expecting a SP experience like the previous Wolfenstein games.

This is easily my favorite side-scrolling arcade-style action game, and generally one of my favorite games of 2013. I love the well-designed action and platforming mechanics, around which the enemies are designed cleverly and with plenty of variety between levels. The art design is really nice too, except for 5-2, which looks cluttered and uses an ugly shade of mauve. I definitely recommend Volgarr to anyone who wants the challenge of oldschool games, only with the polish afforded by decades of innovation.

The makers got the look and feel of classic arcade SNES style slashers down pat this game has got to be a tip of the hat to Rastan . The controls are smooth, and the irreversibility of jump directions and such give the game a bit of an original-Castlevania-esque feel, where you have to plan your moves a little more carefully. Not recommended. Whenever people ask why I cooled on Overwatch 2, I rarely talk about meta or patches first; I talk about the player behavior. Whenever I come back to ARK, I remember the community problem almost immediately. I expect tilt; I don't expect the culture to be this mean by default. I don't expect online games to be gentle, but there is a huge difference between competitive and rotten, and this community crosses that line a lot.

Without a doubt the best sub game, and overall naval game I've ever played. I had 3 days off here and literally all I did was play this game. I was afk a bit but overall I already have 25 hours, more than I have with many games that I've owned for months, if not years. I've also only played 1 1 2 of the campaigns so far out of 3, plus there are tons of single missions. Apparently there is a really amazing mod community as well, allowing you to play as the Red October, and just the Soviets additional subs in general. I hope there are additional weapon mods as well, since I would love to add that as some extra variety. As of now, what they have anti-ship tomahawks, land attack tomahawks, 500lbs anti-ship missiles, a couple generations of guided and unguided torpedoes, and decoys. I know this makes up the bulk of anti-sub warfare, but even more variations of torps or rockets would be cool, even if they aren't 100 historically accurate. There only seems to be about 5-6 mission types, but they are all dynamic and I have't gotten sick of them at all yet. That being said, I would without a doubt pay for at least 2 more mission types, plus MULTIPLAYER if it was released as DLC. Since each mission can easily last a hour, this would make for some really intense PVP with how much depth and realism is modeled.

Alright so after 300 hours I can say the developers have done a great job updating and fixing problems as fast as they arrive. I honestly haven't played game where the developers have been so involved with listening to community and squashing problems as fast as they can. At launch the game wasn't solo-able at all, but they did a good job increasing the base farming and taming rate by 2x. Then you have have 2x harvesting event each weekend making it even easier to farm. They keep further reducing the weight of items and making good changes to your character increasing stats and taking away terrible debuffs Old Age Debuff . I play PvE official with 1 other friend. I manage to play a bit more than him, do to the fact I love these type of games. We both solo built our own Brigs in one day for each Brig during a 2x event. So farming for a decent size boat is pretty easy. It took a few days to build our Galleon only because we slowed down on playing. I managed to farm most of the mats for our galleon during one 2x event with a bear, and elephant. This includes the max amount of gunports and cannons to fill them. Developers managed to decrease the amount of metal on large gunports by 90 making it super easy to build the biggest ship. I had to rewrite this review because its not fair for me to play this game as much as I do and enjoy it as much as I do and keep my review negative. The game gets better week by week. Can't wait for what the future holds for this "Pirate MMO."

good cute game, mods funny, do buy. 10 10

The part people downplay with Teamfight Tactics is how bad the social side gets. The systems are fine; the community is what keeps souring it. A lot of matches feel like people trying a new character get mocked until they stop talking the minute a round slips away. The social feed around the game is nonstop profanity and griefing. That's the part nobody can patch out with a hotfix. It is usually worst in Discord groups.

half the time, i do not think Brawlhalla is ruined by balance alone; the bigger problem is the kind of behavior people tolerate in matches. I hear hateful comments toward certain groups way too often, reporting it rarely seems to change anything Even when you mute one person, somebody else picks up the same behavior. It is the reason i do not recommend it.

For real, I can enjoy Final Fantasy XIV mechanically, but the community keeps sabotaging that. I've had teammates threaten me for refusing to pocket-heal them. And that's what made me uninstall for a while.

For me, Paladins gets ugly fast. There are still way too many games where people from certain countries trigger a parade of bad stereotypes and nobody pushes back. Whenever a girl joins comms, the creepy comments begin immediately. It wears you down quicker than losing ever does.
Best game you can buy !!

Even when Diablo IV is playable, the social atmosphere is rough. Mechanically it's still a solid game, socially it's a mess. I've seen too many lobbies where players with immigrant backgrounds become an excuse for everyone to start punching down. It says a lot when muting everyone feels like the only way to enjoy the game.

whenever i queue solo, Warframe has a decent core game but the chat gets ugly fast with racist and prejudiced comments, the fact that it happens in ranked and casual says everything with the added bonus that people start making threats the moment a match goes sideways on top of that people dogpile new players and call them useless with the added bonus that women get hit with creepy comments the second they use voice chat, it ruins teamwork because the lobby turns into harassment instantly and it is the reason i do not recommend it.

when i think about why Dead by Daylight feels toxic, female players deal with nonstop sexual harassment here, no one should have to mute themselves to avoid that while also there are too many messages threatening revenge for nothing, once threats show up, the whole match feels unsafe then some players openly attack others for where they are from, it is exhausting for anyone who is not part of the majority in chat, so i cannot blame people for quitting which makes the whole thing harder to enjoy.

The problem with Overwatch 2 is mostly the people. Random squads have a rotten baseline vibe. The argument doesn't stay in-game for long; it stops sounding like venting and starts sounding menacing the second someone answers back. It turns a decent game into a chore. After a while you start expecting it before the match even begins.
